stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 2021, researchers from Stanford University documented hydrothermal vents along the Galapagos Rift.
  • A hydrothermal vent is a fissure on the seafloor that releases superheated, mineral-rich water.
  • The water emerging from these vents can reach temperatures of 400 degrees Celsius.
  • The combination of extreme heat and dissolved minerals makes the water inhospitable to most deep-sea organisms.
  • Specialized chemosynthetic microbes are able to thrive near the vents.
stem: The student wants to explain why hydrothermal vents are inhospitable to most deep-sea organisms. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Though hydrothermal vents are inhospitable to most deep-sea organisms, some chemosynthetic microbes can thrive there.
Hydrothermal vents are fissures on the seafloor that release superheated, mineral-rich water.
The water near hydrothermal vents is inhospitable to most deep-sea organisms because it combines extreme heat (up to 400 degrees Celsius) with dissolved minerals.
The hydrothermal vents along the Galapagos Rift are inhospitable to most deep-sea organisms and were documented by researchers in 2021.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It explains why the vents are inhospitable by citing the combination of extreme heat and dissolved minerals.

Choice A states the vents are inhospitable but offers no explanation. Choice B defines vents without addressing inhospitability. Choice D states inhospitability and date of discovery but offers no reason.
